channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: 2K And FIFA Came Close To Agreement On Game Before Talks Broke Down—REPORT
item.content:encoded:
How close did FIFA and 2K come to signing a deal for a brand-new simulation soccer game? According to a new report, the ball was nearly over the goal line.

According to Sports Gamers Online (SGO), talks between FIFA and 2K “progressed to the point where both sides were negotiating contract terms, licensing agreements, and potential game modes.”

SGO sources claim that 2K was looking for a 10-year licensing deal that includes rights to the FIFA World Cup along with the FIFPRO license for clubs and players. Modes pitched included an ownership mode that would be a “lighter version of Football Manager”.

What Happened With The Deal?

Talks eventually broke down due to FIFA’s desire for “faster, more profitable opportunities.” FIFA wanted to capitalize, per the report, on the World Cup, which included the new FIFA World Cup: Launch Edition with Netflix.

  • Undisputed’s Future: What CEO Ash Habib wishes he had done differently.

A possible FIFA 2K game has been known about for quite some time, with talks dating back a few years. Work had even begun on assembling a team to develop a game to compete with the likes of EA Sports FC.

As with any rumor, please take this news with a grain of salt unless confirmed by the parties or Insider Gaming can corroborate with our own sources.

Insider Gaming has reached out to 2K, its owner Take-Two Interactive, and FIFA for comment on the claims. Should responses be received, they will be added to this story.

In other news, read our thoughts on the F1 25: 2026 Season Pack and whether it’s worth picking up while you wait for next year’s game. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Riot Games Gifts Newlywed Couple Custom League of Legends Plaque
item.content:encoded:
A couple who met through League of Legends have received a custom wedding gift from Riot Games.

Twitter user lronelia revealed she had contacted Riot Support to try and get a message for her sister and fiancé ahead of their wedding on Saturday ,June 13.

The couple met almost 12 years ago through League of Legends, meaning sometime around Season 4, and have continued playing ever since.

However, Riot went one step further and sent a custom, personalised LoL-themed gift for the soon-to-be-newlyweds.

The couple received a message featuring a picture of LoL couple Xayah and Rakan which reads:

“Congratulations on your wedding! ‘Magic is in the heart. Magic is in the Rhythm.’

“Hoping that your hearts dance to the same rhythm, just like their recall.”

The couple were then also sent a wooden box with their in-game usernames and the same Xayah and Rakan picture on the front.

The couple are currently ranked in Gold and Emerald, so don’t believe the claims that you need to reach Challenger to get married.

While a picture of what was inside hasn’t been posted online, the Twitter user already peeked inside.

The box contains a plaque of Summoner’s Rift, LoL’s primary map, with a message on top that says “we met here” along with the date that the couple first added each other in-game.

There were also personal messages from various Rioters inside, the support team were probably delighted to not get a message about toxicity for once.

What’s the best thing you’ve ever seen a games publisher do for fans of its game? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord. 

The couple have seen hundreds of updates to the game, and another is coming with new League of Legends champion Locke, releasing soon.

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Director Says Streaming RPGs is a ‘Bit of a Crisis’
item.content:encoded:
Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director recently discussed how streaming games like their RPG can be a problem for the developers.

It has already been a few days since the highly anticipated final installment of the remake trilogy was revealed, and players have already begun raising all sorts of questions. We have already been informed about cut content, and now the director has addressed how streaming their title can be an issue.

Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Director Discusses the Problem with Streaming Games

In a recent interview with 4Gamer (via Automaton), Naoki Hamaguchi, the director of Final Fantasy 7 Revelation, addressed the challenges of streaming RPGs. He explained that this issue can be particularly problematic for an RPG like theirs, as players might feel satisfied simply by watching it stream, leading them to miss out on actually playing it themselves. He explains that “This is a bit of a crisis for the work itself, or rather, it’s not something game creators can wholeheartedly celebrate.”

Hamaguchi clarified his stance on streaming, emphasizing that he does not dismiss its value. He discussed the challenge of video games being perceived primarily as stories to be enjoyed by watching rather than playing. He added, “if people watch a stream and it makes them think What would I do in that situation? or How would I experiment with that?, then hopefully they’ll be inspired to try it themselves.” Hamaguchi also discussed adapting to changing times, which he has been trying to do across all 3 installments of the series.

In other news, Square Enix has announced a brand new HD-2D Final Fantasy game. Additionally, Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director has revealed that the game does have choices, but will feature a ‘singular ending’. What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director stating that streaming RPGs are a bit of a crisis? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis being canon to the survivor trilogy. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Cuts Key Content From The Original
item.content:encoded:
The developers have confirmed what content has been cut from the original for Final Fantasy 7 Revelation.

The final part of the Final Fantasy 7 Remake trilogy has been finally revealed at the latest Summer Game Fest event, and players cannot wait to see what’s in store for them for the conclusion. However, it is a remake in the end, and the third part was unable to incorporate all the elements of the original title.

Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Won’t Feature Bone Village

The confirmation about Final Fantasy 7 Revelation’s cut content comes from director Naoki Hamaguchi’s latest interview with RPGsite (via Gamerant). In one of the questions, Hamaguchi was directly asked whether players will be able to go to Bone Village in the final part of the trilogy, to which the director responded, stating: “Bone Village itself is not included, but North Wood and the Temple of Ancients, we’ll be able to revisit.” 

For those unaware, Bone Village was a field map in the original Final Fantasy title, which was found in the Icicle Area. The map was used to dig up rare items, and a digging minigame also provided players with important items. The map was also used to dig up the Lunar Harp, which was required to access the Forgotten City. However, all is not lost, as the snowboarding minigame and other minigames will return in the next part.

In other news, Square Enix has confirmed that there is a new English voice actor for Sephiroth in Final Fantasy 7 Revelation. Additionally, Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director has revealed that the game does have choices, but will feature a ‘singular ending’. What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Revelation’s cut content? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about 1666: Amsterdam developer admitting to generative AI use. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
